description: The Ghee Hin Kongsi was a Chinese secret society in Singapore and Malaya, formed in 1820. It was part of the Hongmen overseas network and provided mutual aid for Chinese migrants, aiming to overthrow the Qing dynasty. The society was involved in violent conflicts with rival secret societies and was suppressed by colonial authorities from the 1890s.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Ghee Hin Kongsi ... was a secret society in Singapore and Malaya
country_links.context: The Ghee Hin Kongsi was a secret society in Malaya, indicating its presence in what is now Malaysia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Ghee Hin Kongsi ... was a secret society in Singapore ... Their main lodge in Singapore was located on Lavender Street
country_links.context: The Ghee Hin Kongsi was a secret society in Singapore, with its main lodge located on Lavender Street.
